name: First Row and Column as Markers
summary: Encode which rows/columns must zero into the first row and column, then apply them â€” O(1) extra space.
visualizationIntent: init: the matrix is shown with its first row and first column marked special (the marker band); pass 1(mark): scanning the interior, any zero writes a 0 marker into that cell's row-head and column-head; pass 2(apply): interior cells whose row-head or column-head is 0 flip to result (0); pass 3/4: the first row and first column themselves are zeroed if needed; return: matrix holds the fully zeroed result using no extra space.

lineExplanations.5: Track separately whether the first column must be zeroed.
lineExplanations.7: If this row's first cell is zero...
lineExplanations.8: Remember to zero the whole first column later.
lineExplanations.9: Walk the columns after the first.
lineExplanations.10: If this interior cell is zero...
lineExplanations.11: Mark its row by zeroing the row's head cell.
lineExplanations.12: Mark its column by zeroing the column's head cell.
lineExplanations.15: If this cell's row-head or column-head is a marker zero...
lineExplanations.17: If the shared corner marks the first row...
lineExplanations.20: If the first column was flagged earlier...

syntaxExplanations.1: class Solution: the wrapper class the platform runs.
syntaxExplanations.2: def setZeroes(self, matrix): the method receiving the matrix.
syntaxExplanations.3: len(matrix) is the number of rows.
syntaxExplanations.4: len(matrix[0]) is the number of columns.
syntaxExplanations.5: first_col_zero = False is a boolean flag.
syntaxExplanations.6: for row in range(rows) iterates the row indices.
syntaxExplanations.7: matrix[row][0] == 0 checks the row's first cell.
syntaxExplanations.9: range(1, cols) skips column 0, the marker column.
syntaxExplanations.11: matrix[row][0] = 0 marks this row in its head cell.
syntaxExplanations.12: matrix[0][col] = 0 marks this column in its head cell.
syntaxExplanations.15: matrix[row][0] == 0 or matrix[0][col] == 0 tests either marker.
syntaxExplanations.17: matrix[0][0] == 0 tests the shared corner marker.
syntaxExplanations.18: for col in range(cols) walks the first row.
syntaxExplanations.20: if first_col_zero tests the saved flag.
syntaxExplanations.21: for row in range(rows) walks the first column.
syntaxExplanations.23: return matrix hands back the modified grid.
